# HV Clearance-Aware Placement: Solving the 33% Routing Completion Wall

**Date:** 2026-06-23
**Status:** complete
**Generated by:** ce-ideate (run 48227a1c)

## Objective

Improve PCB placement to unlock routing completion from 33% (8/24 nets) to 100%, focusing on the phased_component_assignment, zone_geometry, and slot_generation stages. The 10 stuck nets are blocked by 6mm HV (high-voltage, IEC 60335-1) creepage clearances around power components Q1, Q2, D1, D2.

## Grounding Context

**Codebase:** Temper deterministic PCB autorouter (JAX/Python). 22-stage pipeline. Placement lives in `phased_component_assignment`, `zone_geometry`, `slot_generation`, `zone_assignment`. Zones are 1D horizontal strips. Slot generation ignores HV exclusion zones. Placement scoring is HPWL-only with zero routing awareness. HV nets locked to F.Cu.

**Past learnings:** Per-stage DRC fence pattern exists for placement-stage HV invariant; Stage 2 channel analysis decomposed into 8 micro-stages but not wired back to placement; routability-retry feedback contracts exist (rewind to placement when completion < 50%); `NetClassRules.safety_category` is SSOT for HV/LV classification; `placement.channels.json` sidecar exists for channel capacity communication.

**External:** Room-based constraint scoping (commercial EDA tools), congestion-driven feedback loops (VLSI/FPGA), inflate footprints by clearance distance (motion planning), arterial pre-routing (urban planning), min-cut bottleneck detection (network flow).

## Survivors (7 ideas)

### 1. HV/LV Pre-Placement Guard Strip

**Axis:** Zone topology surgery
**Confidence:** High | **Complexity:** Low (~100 lines)

Insert a pre-placement stage that reads `NetClassRules.safety_category`, partitions components into HV and LV buckets, computes a 6mm guard strip between them, then runs standard placement on two separated domains. HV components cluster near board edge; LV occupy interior. The guard strip becomes a known-empty routing corridor. Eliminates inter-zone creepage mixing by construction.

### 2. Ghost-Pad Injection: HV Clearance as Physical Obstacles

**Axis:** HV footprint inflation
**Confidence:** High | **Complexity:** Low (~50 lines)

Inject 6mm-radius "ghost pads" at every HV pin position into `_reserve_slots` in phased_component_assignment.py. Makes the placer treat creepage as physical obstruction. Decouples HV safety from every downstream stage. Minimal code change with maximal impact.

### 3. Channel-Aware Placement Scoring

**Axis:** Placement scoring with routing foresight
**Confidence:** Medium-High | **Complexity:** Medium (~150 lines)

Wire decomposed Stage 2 channel analysis (obstacle maps, occupancy grids, bottleneck scores from `placement.channels.json`) into `_place_optimize` scoring. Add routability penalty term for slots in high-congestion cells. Each placement iteration now directly optimizes for routing success.

### 4. Seed Filtering by Channel Bottleneck Map

**Axis:** Iterative feedback loop
**Confidence:** High | **Complexity:** Very Low (~30 lines)

Pre-filter placement seed candidates by sampling the bottleneck map at component positions. O(1) per seed. Discard seeds that place components into known congestion zones. Statistical compounding over retries. Profiler can dynamically adjust strictness.

### 5. Pre-Route HV Clearance Obstacle Expansion

**Axis:** HV footprint inflation
**Confidence:** High | **Complexity:** Low-Medium (~80 lines)

Expand all HV pad geometries by 6mm in the obstacle map before routing. The router operates on a verifiably conservative model — it cannot route into creepage-violating space. Eliminates the failure mode by construction. C-space inflation from motion planning, proven technique.

### 6. Capacitated Routing Graph with Min-Cut Bottleneck Detection

**Axis:** HV constraint relaxation / Feedback loop
**Confidence:** Medium | **Complexity:** Medium-High (~200 lines)

Build capacitated grid-graph over board; edge capacity drops near HV components. Route nets as flows. When a net fails, compute min s-t cut of residual graph for exact bottleneck geometry. Report "Q1 at (22.2, 15.0) and D1 at (30.5, 25.0) create a 4mm gap that needs 6mm" instead of "10 nets stuck."

### 7. Consume Existing Isolation Slots in Slot Generation

**Axis:** Zone topology surgery / HV footprint inflation
**Confidence:** High | **Complexity:** Low (~40 lines)

Config already defines `isolation_slots` (1.5mm milled cutouts between gate/HV pins, extending creepage path per IEC 62368-1 Annex G) but `zone_aware_slot_generation.py` ignores them. Add isolation slot geometry as slot-blocking polygons and propagate effective clearance reduction to DRC oracle. Reclaims ~4mm per slot.

## Roadmap (recommended order)

| Priority | Idea | Why First |
|----------|------|-----------|
| 1 | Ghost-Pad Injection (#2) | Lowest code change, highest direct impact, zero new infrastructure |
| 2 | Seed Filtering (#4) | Cheapest compute, highest compounding, trivially testable |
| 3 | Channel-Aware Scoring (#3) | Leverages existing Stage 2 infrastructure, makes placement routing-aware |
| 4 | HV/LV Guard Strip (#1) | Structural guarantee, catches what ghost pads miss |
| 5 | Clearance Obstacle Expansion (#5) | Eliminates routing-level HV checks entirely |
| 6 | Isolation Slots in Slot Gen (#7) | Consumes existing unused config, reclaims channels |
| 7 | Min-Cut Bottleneck Detection (#6) | Diagnostic infrastructure for future optimization |
